speaker: MATT
raw_text: Also, for those who may not remember from last week, we are in a very interesting month of Critical Role, as the party has been split due to a circumstance where, in the city of Vasselheim, the party, chasing after a hydra that seemed to be harrying the outside walls of the city and slaying it, got in the way and accidentally broke the laws of the Vasselheim by hunting and slaying when they weren't approved to do so. Being called skags by a band of hunters that works for the Slayer's Take guildhall. A guild that pretty much regulates most hunting of monsters and creatures within the vicinity of Vasselheim. So, as opposed to going up and dealing with charges at the seat of justice, they instead were given the option by the head of the guild hall to possibly join, but in order to do so, they would have to fulfill a contract and prove themselves worthy of being a member of the guild. And the guild master, our fantastic Huntmaster Vanessa Cyndrial, hand-picked two different groups, splitting up the twins, and they have all set out to return the next day to join, and figure out what their contract will be. So, this week we have playing: we have Grog. We have Vex'ahlia. We have Percy. We have the shortly arriving here Scanlan, who has been fighting L.A. traffic.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And welcome back. So, a couple things I want to get off the bat to you guys. Our subscription giveaways this evening. For every hundred subscribers, we are giving away special care packages with D&D books signed by the Critical Role crew, as well as a signed photo, comics, other goodies. So the next one will be at 5,300, we will do another giveaway. Remember, you have to be in the chat room to be able to acquire one of these packages. So tune in to get something. Keep that in mind.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Now, after defeating the hydra, being called on their law breakage in Vasselheim, have been tasked to return the next morning, as separate groups, to figure out what contract is being given to them and what members of their team await them as they arrive. So, you guys have taken the evening to rest. The next morning has come about. You find yourselves awakening within your respective tavern rooms, the members of Vox Machina. What preparation would you like to embark in this morning for whatever mysterious trek awaits you?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. So as you and Vax'ildan get together at the bar, you have the strongest form of nasty Vasselheim liquor that they could possibly muster. It looks somewhat murky, it has elements of blue and gray to it. The barkeep assured you that this is-- "This is what you're looking for to wake up in the morning, trust me."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Do they have a weapons closet?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh shit, I thought we were in the guildhall.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Not yet. You can go there if you want to, but if there is anything you want to do before arriving at the guildhall, that's up to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Do you want anything from the tavern before we leave?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What difference does it make? I want to take a shot with my brother.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Interesting. I will say, due to time constraints, you would probably have to skip a decent breakfast to do this. So, be aware you may be hungry later on.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: And we've got that leather from that weird bat creature from the Underdark.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm sure it doesn't smell bad.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Is there any metal canisters in there, of any kind?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I mean, I've got my hooded lantern, but you can't have that. It's dark in places.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I bet Tiberius has metal canisters, he's got so many canisters.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Two pieces of armor. Just armor

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I may take a piece of armor and see if I can put together some sort of frag grenade thing.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I just want to make a--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Just make a canister, put some black powder in there, put the thing together. It doesn't even have a fuse, it's something I can toss and shoot to trigger.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: He looks up as you guys approach. "Very nice you all could finally make it, it appears you are only a grand total of 25 minutes late."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I made a holy hand grenade. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Indeed. All right, as the rest of you gather up, you've had your breakfast, you rested, you make your way towards the Slayer's Take guildhall. Approaching the front steps again, the wooden engraving on the front doors, you step into the main foyer area and are greeted again by Mertin, the gruff looking halfling, who is in the process of thumbing through some books. He has some dried fruit on the side of his chin. He's a little groggy, like he didn't sleep well the night before.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: He comes back after about two minutes with a small handbook and reads through. "Yes, well, it is customary that as a part of these trials, there is a member of the Slayers Take is to accompany you on this journey to make sure that you--"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This would be more of an insight check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Best you can tell. From her posture, the way she walks, there is definitely a, for lack of a better term, a non-arrogant self importance to how she holds herself. But there is something about it that... there is a mild hint of insecurity there.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Could I ask you what your weapon of choice is?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: It took many years to forge it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: At this point Mertin is looking angrily at a small pocket watch that he pulls out and looks at. "This is very unusual. Let me see who is supposed to be leading your group. Hold on just a moment." And he gets up and exits the room briefly.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Anyway. This is a secondary gift. Temporarily lent out for your contracts." Mertin brings up what looks like another satchel. It has a series of metal snaps across the top. These little buttons that snap together and enclose it entirely. It's an over-the-shoulder-type throw. As he opens it and you look inside, it resembles the bag of holding, but it's not quite a bag of holding. "This is used for the transportation of, how do you say, perishable aspects of your quarry. Things that you would harvest from the creature you have been assigned tend to, depending on the creature, rot away within a very short period of time. When placed within this, they should be sustained for a few weeks at the very least. So, please do be very mindful and careful of this. Should it be lost, there is a very small chance you will be able to fulfill your contract. So, who shall be taking this on the journey?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Well, just because I-- I defer to--

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: As the leader, I enjoy being hospitable, and I agree with this small person.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Well Scanlan, if you feel you need it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I already have a couple, so I will gladly give one to somebody who is going to need it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I've got plenty. Take it. If you need it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: I have plenty myself. Thank you very much.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very well. Now that we have this situated. Has everyone gathered the provisions they require for this journey? Are you ready to embark?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I mean, we don't have a body bag for some of these chumps.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: The biscuit was very filling. It was extremely filling.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: You're very welcome. I can get some more. Can I just go to the kitchen and grab some-- no?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "You have now received your gifts and your various required implements for this journey. A contract has just recently come to us from the Crown of Erathis, within this portion of the city. It has been approved for slaying. This will prove quite a challenge. Based on how highly you spoke of your abilities, I will warn you. The quarry is quite dangerous. Should you find yourself in trouble, I wish to remind you that it is better to live and fail, than to be foolish and die. Let me pass on to you the contract that has been selected for you." Then she reaches out and hands this over to you, Percy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (gasps) But I won. I won. Give it to me. What? Please.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: I'm sorry, I don't think it was given to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I already have it. I already have it on my shoulder. It's too late. I'm writing it down.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: I certainly hope you don't lose it during our quest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Vanessa, I have a question. What happens if we don't come back with all of the members we left with?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, then I suppose they would not be allowed into the guildhall."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Mostly for no longer returning to the city of Vasselheim, but that is not our problem. There is danger in this which I should get to, actually."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, please tell us. Where are we going?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As you all leave, the Huntmaster expectantly waiting for you to exit the guildhall, you find yourself out amongst the streets of Vasselheim. With no particular direction in where to find the white dragon in three days.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh god, here we go again.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I mean, he looked actually kind of frightened.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: Was he wearing armor? Like, a red-ish sort of very fantastic armor?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It was hard to focus on his armor with all of his screaming and panting.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: He is very brave. He must have been injured.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, yes, he seems like he would have been had he hadn't--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We tease because we love. He did fine. He fought well. He fought bravely, and he treated us fairly.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Especially when he got burned by Tiberius.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Not the face. Not the face.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: We need to talk about this more on the road, but we should get going.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You are still in the city of Vasselheim. You are in the Quadroads district, which is the most central portion of the city-- it's the Erathis driven area. You are about a 15 minute walk from the nearest walls that exit out into the Vesper Timberlands.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's like fishing line coming out of his face.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm a damn pirate now. Watch out.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Like a 14 year old whose father doesn't love him enough to teach him how to groom himself.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: There's no parental issues over here. None.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: So, can I do that then?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can. You, along with Trinket by your side, you find a small space--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I can't find his exact location, but I can...

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: How far away are we, from the wood, at this point?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "No, no one around here likes a dragon proper. At least, none that I've come across. No, I swears it. I swears it. I saw this one. I was up north, about two days' travel northwest of here via the Glasswalk Road. That's two days' travel horseback, mind you."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're not with us, are you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You're with me. We're still wandering about like idiots.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys are in the process of asking around. You'll meet up with them in just a moment.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: Vex, you're very attractive. Use your wiles.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That's my name." He's cleaning a glass.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I've heard many things about your knowledge of dragons.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Y--You've seen it? You've seen the dragon?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I have seen dragons, and they suck.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You hear a couple of snickers down the bar and he goes, "Shut the hell up!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Unless you like them. Do you like them?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "I was heading to Muldire to, you know, visit my brother and some of my family lives up in that northern area. It's freezing cold, too chilly for my tastes but-- I was going to visit and no more than two days up there, a nasty blizzard blows in out of nowhere, just starts wrecking the entire roadside, slick with ice. We decide to go ahead and make camp, me and a couple of the others who were journeying with me. We head off the road, set up a small camp, throw up some tents, and thankfully, at least within the canopy of the forest, it's easy enough to escape the barrage of the elements, but it was a very, very cold night. Our fire began to go out, and we were a little worried."

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: Vex, you're very attractive. Use your wiles.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That's my name." He's cleaning a glass.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I've heard many things about your knowledge of dragons.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Y--You've seen it? You've seen the dragon?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I have seen dragons, and they suck.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You hear a couple of snickers down the bar and he goes, "Shut the hell up!"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Unless you like them. Do you like them?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, no one around here likes a dragon proper. At least, none that I've come across. No, I swears it. I swears it. I saw this one. I was up north, about two days' travel northwest of here via the Glasswalk Road. That's two days' travel horseback, mind you."

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Before it can plummet, the creature then closes its jaws, snatching down on that worg, and biting it in two. Only one fell stroke. As the pieces of ice clatter to the ground, the rest of the worgs scatter. I believe that dragon saved our lives that night."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That's a giant dagger, if I've ever seen one.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Thank you." He re-sheathes it, a little blush to his cheeks. "I prepared myself to accept possible defeat at these terrifying beasts. Then a secondary roar breaks out. This time not from around, but above. Incoming, streaking out of the clouds above, I see, believe me, a dragon. Wing-span huge, claws and teeth gnashing--"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I would never have guessed that's where that was going. White dragon?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "White as the snow that encased the skyline. It streaks down and snatch! Takes one of the worgs in its jaws. Lifts up the worg, screeching about angrily. It throws it into the air, and from its mouth cascades a blistering, freezing cascade of ice and sleet. The worg itself freezes in mid-air."

speaker: MATT
raw_text: And once again, the horse travel wasn't two days of straight gallop. It was--

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: I think he's adorable, and it would be really nice to have him along.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: You guys, it's so beautiful to see the friendship that you have. If I had that with another person-- say, Aldor-- it would be--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh God. Do you have any Clydesdales or giant horses for me?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Have you thought of getting a cat, by any chance?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Just a suggestion. I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I think we could... probably facilitate. I can't say that it'll have much of a shelf life, depending on how much you ride it, but--"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's what people say about me. That's fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He pulls out a very, very nice looking warhorse that looks like it's designed to carry very heavy soldiers. Still unproven as to whether or not it will be able to carry you, but we shall see.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Two minutes go by.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He gives a very, very long, judgmental, mistrusting look across all five of you.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You know what, Mister Randy? We had our doubts about your--

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, no that's not-- No no no no, he's not my uncle. No-- my uncle is not here, he's just-- this is just one of his--

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: He's going to be-- Uncle Randy is going to eat his hat, isn't he? I got a leadership position! Anyway, can you help us out?

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: We're going into the cold-- we're going into a blizzard, can you believe that? And you know how much I hate the cold, but we do need to be provisioned, so what do we have in the crates that we can help...?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You embark on the Glasswalk Road, which forms right on the outskirts of the Braving Grounds. You are let through the front gates of the outside of the Vesper Timberlands, and this is the first time you've actually seen a formal road that is carved and at least traversed enough that the Timberland hasn't completely consumed it. You can still see various tree roots and branches are progressively, almost angrily, pushing in towards this gate, but you get the feeling based on the cuts that whoever goes through here-- at least some people go through here, they keep it relatively well-kept for travel up north. You ride out into it and the canopy almost completely encloses it, so it's like a tunnel of very dense tree, vine, and leaf structure. You push forward out into the cold air and begin to ride for the remainder of the day. A good eight or nine hours hit before the chill begins to grow. The sun begins to find itself setting across the far mountains, and the forest around you grows darker and darker still.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, we're loaded up. We've got horses, we've got healing potions, we've got--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Loaded for bear, as it were.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to go, one more time to the Braving Grounds, Grog, and just--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Why don't we slay the dragon first, and then--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Since we're on a time crunch, Scanlan.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're right. You're right, there is a--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I haven't finished my training montage yet. Got to run through the snow, got to do pullups on trees.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, we go west along the Glass... Hand... Road.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. You guys get off of the main road, as that's probably not a very good area to be out in the open. You find, after about a five to ten minute walk, a small opening and along the way you find a tree that looks viable. There's a small slope of hill that heads up to an upper platform area maybe about five to ten feet up, and a small opening that you think would be a fitting place to put a fire that wouldn't be in danger of setting the nearby vegetation aflame. You go ahead and start looking for firewood. Go ahead and make an athletics roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: I think we should make camp! I-- should we make camp? Yes! We should make camp!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I have a firewood bundle that we can make a fire with.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: There's also lots of wood around, Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And I have an axe. Let's do that instead. Can I find a really tiny tree and start hacking away at it?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, tiny. We don't need to kill, like, Grandpa Tree. We kill Baby Tree.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Don't-- how about don't tell Keyleth?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. After a good 15, 20 minute period-- relatively brief for most folk-- you come back with a nice, hefty stack of firewood from the driest small tree you could find.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, tiny. We don't need to kill, like, Grandpa Tree. We kill Baby Tree.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Don't-- how about don't tell Keyleth?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You guys get off of the main road, as that's probably not a very good area to be out in the open. You find, after about a five to ten minute walk, a small opening and along the way you find a tree that looks viable. There's a small slope of hill that heads up to an upper platform area maybe about five to ten feet up, and a small opening that you think would be a fitting place to put a fire that wouldn't be in danger of setting the nearby vegetation aflame. You go ahead and start looking for firewood. Go ahead and make an athletics roll.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: About two hours into nightfall, you can see a gentle bit of moonlight begin to appear off the distant horizon to the northeast, from your position. It's hard to see because the trees are so thick, but you take a moment to walk through, and you can see little bits of the moonlight peeking.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. After a good 15, 20 minute period-- relatively brief for most folk-- you come back with a nice, hefty stack of firewood from the driest small tree you could find.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Throwing the wood down, you begin to set up small stones into a fire pit establishment, and begin building a fire as the entirety of the sun disappears behind the distant mountain range. At this point, the temperature has gotten extremely cold, and most of you begin to pull up your winter cloaks and get your bedrolls ready for an evening's bit of rest. Is there anything in particular that you guys want to do before the night and sleep take you?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: I would like to go out. Has the moon risen?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The ranger, who is very well-experienced in surviving in woodland areas, starts the fire that had taken you a good five, six minutes of aggressively fighting against the water-soaked wood of this woodland. (snaps) She lights the flames.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Oh, you do? Oh, yours is nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, but we don't have any ice-resistant things, outside of that damn--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Or fire spells. Fire spells would be very--

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I have a fire spell.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: That's how I took down the worg. So, anyway, we were in the forest--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: How powerful is it? Because I can conjure something and it would be very-- too large for this particular--

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: I mean, I think a Fireball would not help here. I can do Firebolt, but there's really no use until we get there.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do we not have a fire going?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys look over and Grog is sitting there, rubbing wood together.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, I'm probably getting so close. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fine. Take all the joy out of everything.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: About three, four hours go by--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: A dragon. (sighs) I wonder if it was white.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Really? Mine was an asshole too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: He was. He was a dark wizard. He was awful.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, wow. Mine's just a jerk.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: He kept me in a cage.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Getting a bit personal here around the fire.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Sorry. I thought we were bonding.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Thank you. God, what is this?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Disgusting. Grog found it. I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Is it wood? All right, well at least it's elm.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys munch and awkwardly make your way through conversation through the evening, eventually your watch comes to a close, and you guys are able to rest. Percy, you take up second watch.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You are actually going first. Everyone else, you're currently in the process of waking up so there's going to be a round before it gets to the rest of you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Cool. So. Initiative rolls for everyone--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There will be a mild surprise round here except for Grog, but we have initiatives from here. Percy, what you got?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh God. Why did we all roll terribly.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We'll be dead. We'll be dead before we leave.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh this is wonderful, you're all going to die.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're all going to die, man!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I should have grabbed some of that dessert that's here.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I just had a couple. They're great, and I'm sure Liam could get us some more.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. So. In this regard, Grog.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: 21 points of damage. All right, with that shot as it's getting up and reaches for its club, it gets hold of it and starts bringing it in an arc towards you. You lower the gun again, this time shooting for the other side of its face. With an echoing blasting sound you see the remainder of its lower jaw and head just blown out the back of it. Its eyes roll back. (cheering)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Out of idle curiosity, if I try to make a break for it they would get two attacks of opportunity against me, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes. Yes, they are harrying you pretty heavily right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, that doesn't make my life any better does it. No, never mind then.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Actually, you know what? I'm pulling an Action Surge. Two more shots.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm shooting him two more times while he's on the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Same thing with a plus ten to hit with a Sharpshooter.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Advantage to your shot because he's still on the ground.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 23 hits. Go ahead and roll damage on that.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Let's see. Sorry, trying to do this... five plus 16, so 21 points of damage.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That concludes your turn. It's now their turn. All you low initiative rolling motherfuckers got to wait! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. As you (whoosh) swing the axe around, it clips across the chest of the orc who is almost pushed off his feet from the sheer force. The axe swings, slams into the front throat, chest area of the dire wolf with so much force that it cuts clean through leaving this open wound where its main veins just begin to pour its lifeblood.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The dire wolf just gurgles for a second before slumping onto the ground, motionless. With the inertia carrying through its body it slams into the other orc, who actually has a small buckler shield that manages to catch. It shatters most of the leather and metal that encompasses the piece, getting stuck into his forearm and he yells in your face with this kind of guttural shout and he pulls his own arm off the blade of the axe as you withdraw it.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's still-- it's probably like a good two, three in the morning.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: I would like to put my hands on the dire wolf and just thank it, for no other reason than I respect its life.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I don't think he's going to answer.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: You know, it's not for you, Percy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, you take a moment and concentrate and, for a six mile radius-- currently no dragon.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Still? It's still-- oh right, it's towards a mountain.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We've got another day's travel, it's got to be 20 miles away.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Should we keep sleeping, or should we just pack it up now and go?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Eventually you all complete your evening's rest, the rest of the watches go without incident, in the morning you wake up and the cold air was pretty rough for a lot of you. Thankfully the fire was there to help, but you're-- you get the feeling that the further north you travel, the weather's going to become an issue, even with the warm clothing you currently have on. All right. So you guys step back to where your horses are currently--

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, sleepytime is the official name for it! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: Sleepytime it is. Thank you so much.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to sleepytime, good night, all.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Is it-- is it still my watch?

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: And we can heal up, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, so the rest of you guys, you go ahead and take your full evening's rest, at which point your hit points and spells are restored. And Grog, go ahead and make a percentage roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah! Come on, you big bearded bastard! 58.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But you've still got that nice scruff, a good like, y'know, half an inch to an inch of whatever that is pushing through.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Dire wolf blood all over my feet didn't stimulate the growth at all?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: (laughs) And so as you're on your warhorse, which, as you can tell the more and more you ride on it the more and more it's starting to slow. Thankfully you're keeping pace with Trinket, it's having a hard time keeping a goliath on its back for extensive riding.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Being fully asleep, I cast fart. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: The irony is, I was going to recommend Dust of Tracelessness, but that works too.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, that works too. Can you--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: They would never follow that, so.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: What do you want to do, my love. Dear. Whoever you are.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh yes, Dust of Tracelessness. He's got this stuff, we can cover our tracks.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: We slept already, we're on the horses.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And I take the Dust of Tracelessness and I, in the back.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do we ever run out of that stuff?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it is, it's really nice.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: A few hours of-- a few hours of travel and--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Why? Because Tiberius can fly, why the hell did we give them the carpet? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's all right, we don't need the crutch.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: And so anyway, so, when I first met Aldor--

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: -- he was hired to be my bodyguard and it was so obvious that he was so capable when he walked in the room--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How did he react when you first told him of your feelings?

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: I mean, it was not a good evening, but he was paid to be there so he couldn't leave, so that was, well, that was good for me--

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: It's not, it didn't work today, but it could work tomorrow or the day after.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god. Can we make an agreement. I kill you, you kill me?

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: And suddenly he disappears, it's so funny that--

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right, so as you guys pull off the side of the road and use the cover of the forest to help from the snow that's beginning to hit even harder, you gather there is in this vicinity an ever waxing-and-waning blizzard that pours through. You can see a localized area that is not really moving. You wait like a good 20, 30 minutes to see if there's any movement but the blizzard is not shifting from its location.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: And after that, he never saw--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Somehow you made that less sexual than I could've ever imagined. Is there-- Grog, what's the term for when your penis is inverted? The opposite of a boner?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, that's what I've got now.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: Anyway, I was really certain after that moment that we would be together forever and I never saw him again.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You know men are fickle and when I-- I'll have a talk with him.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: He kept the armor, so he has feelings. Let's just move on.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You take the stone and your fatigue fades. You find yourself adjusting well to the cold climate, thanks to your gift from your new leader and--

speaker: MATT
raw_text: So in following Vex through the forest, weaving back and forth, about an hour of travel in you-- I know you gotta get going soon. How soon do you have to go?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh boy. Something out there. Nope.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That is lower than my passive perception, though. Which is 21.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And plus five-- no, that's it, just 21. But it's a 12. For right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's a 12? Okay. You keep an eye out as you continue to move forward.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, but I get to roll twice though, because it's my favored terrain?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's true, you do. Advantage on that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARY
preceding_turns.text: When the cameraman is laughing at you, you know it's a good roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's-- no, that's not that bad-- It's 15.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The things you learn watching Critical Role, ladies and gentlemen.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Pushing forward about another hour and a half, two hours, you eventually make it through the forest, dodging a few other areas where clustered vines and almost thick brambles begin to close off portions of the passage. Without Vex's help on this it would be a very difficult element to traverse, but you manage to find areas you can cut through, and where the weak points of these various interwoven nets of vinework are. You push through until the forest comes to an end at the base of a giant tree-covered mountain that is currently coated in snow and ice. Looking about, you can see that the highest spire is obscured by the very blizzard clouds ahead. There is heavy snowfall in this vicinity and your vision is semi-obscured past a certain area. Only because of Vex's keen sight are you able to make out the details of that mountain disappearing into the blizzard.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: Add it, or take away? Because it's negative.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For you it'd be taking away from your stealth roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so. Though clumsy in form, the group is able to pull you far enough away where they cover your bases on this, and this glowing entity doesn't seem to take notice of your passage as it slowly fades into the stone ruins once again. You press on towards the mountain--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Should we go check that glowing entity out?

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: Do I-- oh, do I know? I have an arcana, or would it be nature, or?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For this, I would say go ahead and make a religion check, actually.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, good, three. I don't know, it's 11.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's spooky and you don't like it.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: I don't like that so let's keep going.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You-- I'd say for this and since you know mountain ranges pretty well, go ahead and make a wisdom check on this, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: Does it look like we can climb the mountain, or is it just too sheer?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead and make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, good, this is my best. Oh! Nope, it's a nine. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And you've seen many pictures of them. Seeing one up close? They're a lot bigger than you thought they'd be.

preceding_turns.speaker: FELICIA
preceding_turns.text: Very large mountain, guys, I'm not sure-- as a leader I'd like to invite you guys to evaluate whether we could possibly climb this mountain.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I would like to evaluate whether or not we could climb this mountain.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's like this red glow beneath the shield, I just kinda look up silently. It's going to be great. Thank you so much, Joe, that's really really sweet. I'm going to use that. Here we have a letter. All right, Sir Drey, about 21 hours ago, block. "Hello there, Critical Role, my name is Drey, and my friends and I have created our own Pathfinder D&D group after religiously watching the show every Thursday." Awesome! "We used Tabletop in which I purchased a battlemap, but we have really old metal figurines, I was wondering if by chance you could point us in the direction of where to get better figurines? I've checked Amazon and a few other comic stores and couldn't find any miniatures. It'd be really a big help if you could point me in the right direction. I know you might not answer back due to busy schedules, but thank you guys for putting on the show and entertaining us Critters. Sincerely, Drey." Thank you, Drey. A lot of these minis come from-- if you can't find it at comic stores nearby there's good websites like Miniature, I think it's Miniature Market. There is--Troll and Toad is one of my mainstays, Troll and Toad has a good selection of minis you can order. eBay is also a good source.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Actually, why don't you hand these out, if you could, thank you so much. This is the next chapter in the One Dwarf Too Many saga.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: As read by Sir Ian McKellen.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I need to get these bound at some point. I feel like I don't work hard enough every time one of these comes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so from Joe, aka jockguy89 to Matt Mercer, "Epic dungeon master needs an epic d20."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's gonna be an engagement d20.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, oh I get my own flashing d20 now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I've seen these, I've seen these, I wanted one. This is one that if it lands on a 20, it flashes a bunch of LEDs on the inside.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It gives you an alarm when you know he's gonna kill a party member.
